newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. FY/interim results were released June 3 (before the window); the only scheduled event is a routine virtual AGM on June 10 (re-elect directors, reappoint PwC, 10% buyback mandate). Today's -3.76% move to HKD 4.86 (YTD -45.9%) appears flow-driven China edge-AI de-rating, not a fundamental event.

sources.label: TipRanks - Horizon Robotics sets June 10, 2026 virtual AGM

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved Horizon up on the under-ownership/edge-AI-purity case and there is zero new fundamental information since June 6; another -3.8% drift with the thesis intact slightly improves entry asymmetry but does not justify a second consecutive rank move.
verdicts.bull: A $9.3B pure-play edge-AI compute platform down ~46% YTD with 10+ OEM design wins (Changan, Chery, 20+ models) and the Starry cockpit-drive fusion chip ramp offers asymmetric rerating room once China ADAS sentiment turns.
verdicts.neutral: No news flow since the June 3 results; the stock is drifting lower with the broader China auto-AI complex, so the setup is unchanged from yesterday's up-rated case and the AGM on June 10 is purely procedural.
verdicts.bear: Persistent price erosion despite design-win evidence suggests structural overhang (pre-IPO holder selldowns, brutal China ADAS price competition from Momenta/NVIDIA-based stacks) and the buyback mandate may not offset supply.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence of Starry fusion-chip volume orders or new flagship OEM wins converting the design-win pipeline into revenue acceleration in H2 2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued shareholder supply and ASP/margin compression in China's ADAS chip price war, leaving the stock a falling knife despite the cheapened valuation.
